docs.title: Part 5: Naming Inconsistencies
docs.text: Issue Details Recommendation dual_rx vs dual_watch Separate tags, correct. dual_rx = two independent receivers; dual_watch = monitoring sub-band. IC-7610 has both; FTX-1 has dual_rx only. Keep both — semantically distinct. agc missing from capabilities [agc] section exists in 5 TOML profiles; hasattr(radio, "set_agc") used in radio_poller:1732. No capability tag. Add agc tag in Phase 2. tuning_step missing get_tuning_step() gated by AdvancedControlCapable (handlers:937). No capability tag. Add tuning_step tag in Phase 2. band_edge missing get_band_edge_freq() gated by AdvancedControlCapable (handlers:951). No capability tag. Add band_edge tag in Phase 2. xfc missing get_xfc_status() gated by AdvancedControlCapable (handlers:958). No capability tag. Add xfc tag in Phase 2. System methods overloaded on power_control get_system_date, get_system_time, get_utc_offset, get_ref_adjust, get_civ_transceive all gated by AdvancedControlCapable. Could share power_control or need a new system tag. Consider system_settings tag or keep under power_control.

docs.title: Part 6: Protocol Class to Capability Tag Mapping
docs.text:
These Protocol classes can be replaced by tag checks in Phase 2:

Protocol Class Equivalent Tag(s) Sites AdvancedControlCapable Multiple (see tables above) ~63 AudioCapable audio ~12 ScopeCapable scope ~16 MetersCapable meters ~8 PowerControlCapable power_control ~8 LevelsCapable af_level, rf_gain, squelch ~6 DualReceiverCapable dual_rx ~4 TransceiverStatusCapable tx ~1

Total isinstance sites: ~118 Total hasattr sites: ~22 Grand total to migrate in Phase 2: ~140 sites

docs.title: Part 7: Recommendations for Phase 2 (#433)
docs.text:
1. Replace all isinstance(radio, AdvancedControlCapable) with specific tag checks. Each site maps to a distinct capability tag (see tables above). This is the highest-value change — it turns a single monolithic gate into fine-grained per-feature gating.

  2. Replace hasattr(radio, "method") with tag checks. The hasattr pattern is fragile (typos, renames). Tag-based gating is the canonical pattern.

  3. Replace other Protocol isinstance checks (AudioCapable, ScopeCapable, etc.) with tag checks where the radio has a .capabilities set. Keep runtime_helpers.py hybrid validation as-is (it handles radios that lack profile-based capabilities).

  4. Add new capability tags to capabilities.py and TOML profiles:

  5. agc — AGC mode selection
  6. tuning_step — tuning step control
  7. band_edge — band edge frequency query

  8. xfc — transverter function status

  9. Consider a system_settings tag to group system-level features (date, time, UTC offset, ref adjust, CIV transceive) that are currently lumped under power_control.

  10. Migration order: Start with radio_poller.py (already has self._caps infrastructure), then handlers.py, then cli.py, then sync.py and rigctld/handler.py.

docs.title: Deep Architecture Review: icom-lan
docs.text: Date: 2026-04-09 Reviewer: Claude Opus 4.6 (automated deep review) Commit: 9bc2d1b (main) Source: 99 Python files in src/icom_lan/ (45,034 LOC) Tests: 155 test files

docs.title: Executive Summary
docs.text:
icom-lan is a well-structured, ambitious project that reverse-engineers a proprietary UDP protocol and exposes it through a clean multi-backend architecture. The protocol layer is solid, the decomposition into capability protocols is well-designed, and the test coverage is generally good.

However, there are significant issues in three areas: (1) god modules that have grown past maintainability thresholds, (2) concurrency patterns with subtle race conditions and fire-and-forget risks, and (3) defensive exception handling that silently swallows failures in critical paths. These are typical for a fast-moving project of this complexity and are all fixable.

Overall verdict: needs_attention

docs.title: [Severity: P1] commands.py is a 6830-line god module with 312 functions
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/commands.py Problem: A single flat module containing every CI-V command builder and parser (312 functions, 6830 lines). No internal organization beyond ordering. Every new radio command adds two more functions to this file. Impact: Extremely difficult to navigate, review, or test in isolation. Import times grow linearly. Changes to one command family risk merge conflicts with unrelated work. Fix: Split into commands/ package by command family: commands/frequency.py, commands/mode.py, commands/scope.py, commands/levels.py, commands/system.py, commands/memory.py. Re-export from commands/__init__.py for backwards compatibility.

docs.title: [Severity: P1] radio.py CoreRadio is a 4593-line class with 334 methods
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/radio.py Problem: CoreRadio has 334 methods, 60+ instance attributes initialized in __init__, and directly implements every capability protocol. Despite decomposition of control-phase, civ-rx, and audio-recovery into runtimes, the class itself remains a monolith that mixes lifecycle management, command dispatch, audio control, scope control, dual-receiver routing, VFO management, and state caching. Impact: Any change to CoreRadio risks breaking unrelated functionality. The massive import section (lines 1-300) is itself a code smell. Testing requires mocking dozens of internal attributes. Fix: Continue the runtime decomposition pattern. Extract scope methods into _scope_runtime.py, audio methods into _audio_runtime.py (beyond just recovery), and dual-receiver routing into _dual_rx_runtime.py. CoreRadio becomes a thin composition root.

docs.title: [Severity: P1] TOCTOU race in UDP port allocation
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py:143-150 Problem: The code binds a temporary UDP socket to get a free port, then immediately closes it and hopes the port is still free when the real CI-V/audio transport binds later: 

_civ_sock = _socket.socket(_socket.AF_INET, _socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
_civ_sock.bind((local_bind_host, 0))
_civ_local_port = _civ_sock.getsockname()[1]
_civ_sock.close()  # Port is now free for anyone to grab
Between close() and the actual transport connect() (line 218), another process could steal the port. Impact: Intermittent OSError: Address already in use during connect, especially under high system load or when multiple instances connect simultaneously. Fix: Pass the already-bound socket fd directly to the transport via socket.fromfd() or SO_REUSEADDR, or use local_port=0 and let the transport bind once.

docs.title: [Severity: P1] AudioSubscription.stop() fires untracked task
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/audio_bus.py:106 Problem: stop() is synchronous but needs to call async _remove_subscriber(). It does: 

asyncio.create_task(self._bus._remove_subscriber(self))
This task is never stored, never awaited, and never checked. If the event loop is closing or the task raises, it's silently lost. Impact: Subscriber leak (never removed from bus), audio RX never stops (last subscriber thinks it's gone but bus still has a reference), potential RuntimeWarning: coroutine was never awaited if no event loop is running. Fix: Store the task and await it in __aexit__, or make stop() async. Since __aexit__ already exists and is async, it should call await self._bus._remove_subscriber(self) directly instead of delegating to stop().

docs.title: [Severity: P1] 61 bare assert resp is not None in radio.py
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/radio.py (61 occurrences) Problem: After _send_civ_raw() returns, the code asserts the response is not None: 

resp = await self._send_civ_raw(civ)
assert resp is not None
freq = parse_frequency_response(resp)
If Python is run with -O (optimizations), all asserts are stripped. Even without -O, AssertionError is not a CommandError or TimeoutError, so callers can't catch it meaningfully. Impact: In production with PYTHONOPTIMIZE=1, resp being None passes silently to the parser, causing cryptic TypeError or AttributeError instead of a clear error. The 61 identical patterns also suggest a missing abstraction. Fix: Replace with explicit checks: 
resp = await self._send_civ_raw(civ)
if resp is None:
    raise CommandError("No response for get_freq")
Or better, create _send_civ_expect() that wraps this pattern once.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] _civ_rx.py has 12 broad except Exception blocks
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py (lines 311, 333, 352, 362, 475, 672, 1013, 1023, 1041, 1096, 1104, 1237) Problem: The CI-V receive pump catches Exception broadly and logs at DEBUG level in many places: 

except Exception:
    logger.debug("civ-rx: cache update failed", exc_info=True)
This means programming errors (KeyError, TypeError, AttributeError from wrong frame parsing) are silently swallowed during normal operation. Only visible with DEBUG logging. Impact: Latent bugs in CI-V parsing are invisible. A malformed frame that triggers a real bug will be logged at DEBUG and never noticed. The CI-V stream appears healthy while state updates silently fail. Fix: Catch specific expected exceptions (ValueError, struct.error, IndexError for malformed frames). Log unexpected exceptions at WARNING or ERROR level. Consider a counter metric for parse failures.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] __init__.py exports 150+ symbols including low-level internals
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/__init__.py Problem: The top-level package exports 150+ symbols including build_civ_frame, parse_header, serialize_header, bcd_encode, bcd_decode, CONTROLLER_ADDR, and 40+ scope sub-command functions. These are implementation details, not public API. Impact: Massively inflated public API surface. Any change to internal wire format functions becomes a breaking change. Import from icom_lan import * pulls in an overwhelming number of symbols. Fix: Reduce __all__ to the true public API: create_radio, Radio, capability protocols, exceptions, types/enums, and RadioProfile. Move internal re-exports to a icom_lan.compat or icom_lan.internals module for users who need low-level access.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] _control_phase.py hardcodes "IC-7610" in conninfo
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py:452 Problem: 

conninfo = build_conninfo_packet(
    ...
    radio_name="IC-7610",
    ...
)
The radio name is hardcoded as "IC-7610" regardless of what radio is actually being connected to. Impact: Works for IC-7610 but may cause issues with other Icom radios that validate the radio_name field in the conninfo packet. Misleading in logs and diagnostics. Fix: Use self._host._profile.model or pass the model name through the host protocol.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] ControlPhaseHost protocol uses Any for critical fields
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/_runtime_protocols.py:114,134,167-170 Problem: Several fields in ControlPhaseHost are typed as Any: 

_conn_state: Any        # Should be RadioConnectionState
_audio_codec: Any       # Should be AudioCodec
_audio_stream: Any      # Should be AudioStream | None
_pcm_tx_fmt: Any
_pcm_rx_user_callback: Any
_opus_rx_user_callback: Any
Impact: mypy cannot verify that runtimes use these fields correctly. Type errors in the control phase / audio recovery paths are invisible to static analysis. Fix: Import the proper types (conditionally under TYPE_CHECKING) and use them.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] Excessive getattr() defensive patterns in radio.py
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/radio.py (6 occurrences), _civ_rx.py (20 occurrences), _control_phase.py (6 occurrences) Problem: Code frequently uses getattr(self, "_attr", None) to access attributes that are always set in __init__: 

_rs = getattr(host, "_radio_state", None)  # Always exists
last = getattr(self, "_last_civ_data_received", None)  # Always exists
Impact: Masks bugs — if an attribute is missing it should be a clear AttributeError, not a silent None fallback. Makes the code harder to reason about (reader can't tell if the attribute might genuinely not exist or if it's just defensive coding). Fix: Use direct attribute access for attributes guaranteed by __init__. Reserve getattr() for truly optional attributes (e.g., on objects that might be mocks).

docs.title: [Severity: P2] _connected / _intentional_disconnect backwards-compat shims
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/radio.py:716-737 Problem: CoreRadio maintains property shims that translate between the old boolean flags and the new RadioConnectionState enum: 

@property
def _connected(self) -> bool:
    return self._conn_state == RadioConnectionState.CONNECTED

@_connected.setter
def _connected(self, value: bool) -> None:
    if value:
        self._conn_state = RadioConnectionState.CONNECTED
    ...
Impact: Two parallel representations of the same state. Internal code can set _connected = False and get a different result than setting _conn_state = DISCONNECTED. The setter for _intentional_disconnect has surprising behavior: setting it to False transitions to RECONNECTING, not CONNECTED. Fix: Migrate all internal callers to use _conn_state directly. Remove the property shims. Update tests that rely on the boolean interface.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] web/server.py has 17 getattr() calls suggesting loose coupling
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/web/server.py Problem: The web server uses getattr() extensively to probe the radio object for capabilities: 

getattr(self._radio, "_profile", None)
getattr(self._radio, "state_cache", None)
These suggest the web server doesn't trust the type of self._radio, even though it's typed as Radio. Impact: The web server is effectively untyped — mypy cannot verify any of these accesses. Refactoring the Radio protocol won't produce type errors in the web server. Fix: Use isinstance() checks against capability protocols where appropriate, and direct attribute access for core Radio methods.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] _receive_guid() busy-loops with 30 iterations
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py:426-437 Problem: 

async def _receive_guid(self) -> bytes | None:
    await asyncio.sleep(0.3)
    guid = None
    for _ in range(30):
        try:
            d = await h._ctrl_transport.receive_packet(timeout=0.1)
            if len(d) == CONNINFO_SIZE:
                guid = d[0x20:0x30]
        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
            break
    return guid
This drains up to 30 packets looking for one with a specific size. Packets that don't match are silently discarded. Impact: During high-traffic scenarios (e.g., radio already streaming scope data), important control packets could be consumed and dropped by this loop. The 0.3s sleep + up to 3s of polling adds latency to connection setup. Fix: Use a filtered receive that matches on packet size, or buffer non-matching packets for later processing.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] web/handlers.py is 2399 lines with mixed concerns
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py Problem: Contains ControlHandler (WebSocket command dispatch), ScopeHandler (scope data streaming), AudioBroadcaster (audio fan-out), and AudioHandler (bidirectional audio WebSocket). Each is a substantial class with its own lifecycle, but they're all in one file. Impact: Hard to navigate, test, or modify one handler without risking others. The file will only grow as new WebSocket endpoints are added. Fix: Split into handlers/control.py, handlers/scope.py, handlers/audio.py.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] radio_poller.py uses sentinel dataclasses instead of proper commands
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py Problem: The command queue uses empty dataclasses as sentinel values: 

@dataclass
class EnableScope: pass

@dataclass
class DisableScope: pass
These are put into a generic asyncio.Queue with no type safety. Impact: Minor — it works, but is an ad-hoc command pattern. No compile-time guarantee that the queue consumer handles all command types. Fix: Use a tagged union / enum-based command pattern, or at minimum a Command base class.

docs.title: [Severity: P3] AudioBus.__init__ types radio as Any
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/audio_bus.py:169 Problem: 

def __init__(self, radio: Any, *, jitter_depth: int = 5) -> None:
Impact: No type checking on the radio parameter. Could pass any object. Fix: Type as AudioCapable (with conditional import under TYPE_CHECKING).

docs.title: [Severity: P3] sync.py hardcodes radio_addr=0x98 default
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/sync.py:56 Problem: The synchronous wrapper hardcodes radio_addr=0x98 (IC-7610) as default, while the async CoreRadio defaults to None and uses profile resolution. Impact: Sync users connecting to non-IC-7610 radios must always specify radio_addr. Inconsistent API between sync and async interfaces. Fix: Match the async signature — default to None and let profile resolution handle it.

docs.title: [Severity: P3] ConnectionError and TimeoutError shadow builtins
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/exceptions.py:20,33 Problem: Custom exceptions named ConnectionError and TimeoutError shadow Python builtins of the same name. Impact: Inside any module that imports these, except ConnectionError catches only the icom-lan version, not stdlib ConnectionError. This is a known design choice (documented in the codebase), but still a footgun for contributors. Fix: Consider IcomConnectionError / IcomTimeoutError in a future major version, or at minimum document the shadowing prominently in the exceptions module.

docs.title: [Severity: P3] IcomRadio subclass is essentially empty
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/radio.py:4556-4566 Problem: 

class IcomRadio(CoreRadio):
    """LAN adapter for IC-7610 built on top of the shared executable core."""

    @staticmethod
    async def _flush_queue(transport, max_pkts=200):
        from ._control_phase import ControlPhaseRuntime
        return await ControlPhaseRuntime._flush_queue(transport, max_pkts)

    pass
The entire class is a CoreRadio with one static method override and a pass statement. Impact: The CoreRadio / IcomRadio split suggests a future where CoreRadio is backend-neutral, but currently CoreRadio itself is fully LAN-specific (references UDP transports, ports, etc.). The split is premature. Fix: Either merge back into IcomRadio (since CoreRadio is LAN-specific anyway), or actually make CoreRadio backend-neutral by extracting LAN-specific transport setup.

docs.title: [Severity: P3] Protocol compliance check uses IcomRadio(host="") instances
docs.text:
File: src/icom_lan/radio.py:4574-4593 Problem: 

def _check_protocol_compliance() -> None:
    assert isinstance(IcomRadio(host=""), Radio)
    assert isinstance(IcomRadio(host=""), AudioCapable)
Creates actual radio instances (allocating transports, state caches, etc.) just to check isinstance. Impact: Wasteful; creates real IcomTransport objects. Could fail if constructor has side effects. Not run in tests automatically. Fix: Add this as a proper test in tests/test_radio.py. Use @pytest.mark.parametrize with all protocol types.

docs.title: [Severity: P1] radio_poller.py _execute() is 920 lines with 100+ match branches
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/web/radio_poller.py:657-1577 Problem: Single massive match statement dispatching all radio commands. Each branch has duplicated receiver-switching logic, optimistic state updates, and event publishing. Impact: Impossible to modify without affecting multiple branches. High copy-paste bug risk. Untestable per-command. Fix: Extract to a command dispatcher table: handlers = {SetFreq: self._handle_set_freq, ...}.

docs.title: [Severity: P1] web/handlers.py has 9 nearly-identical _enqueue_rc_* methods
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py:1037-1800 Problem: 9 handler methods (frequency, power, DSP, audio, scope, antenna, system, memory, misc) share identical structure: match on command name, parse params, check capability, build dataclass, queue. 67 similar case/match patterns. Impact: Changes to validation logic must be replicated 9 times. Bugs exist in some methods but not others. Fix: Create a handler registry/factory eliminating structural duplication.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] dict[str, Any] dominates web handler types (32 occurrences)
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/web/handlers.py Problem: All HTTP/WebSocket message handlers use dict[str, Any] instead of TypedDict or Protocol-based message types. No schema validation at type-check time. Impact: Runtime errors on malformed messages, no IDE autocomplete, refactoring hazard. Fix: Create TypedDict classes for each message type.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] Unbounded retransmit buffer in transport
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/transport.py (tx_buffer) Problem: self.tx_buffer: OrderedDict[int, bytes] = OrderedDict() has no size limit. If radio doesn't ACK, buffer grows without bound. Impact: Memory leak under poor network conditions. Fix: Add max_tx_buffer_size and drop oldest when exceeded.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] Packet queue overflow race condition in transport
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/transport.py:580-605 Problem: Between full() check and put_nowait(), another task can drain the queue. The recovery logic doesn't handle this race atomically. Impact: Non-scope packets can be silently dropped during high load. Fix: Use a single atomic operation or wrap both check+put in a lock.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] No lock on connection state transitions
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/_control_phase.py:280-285 Problem: disconnect() checks _conn_state != CONNECTED and then sets DISCONNECTING without a lock. A concurrent connect/watchdog can observe inconsistent state. Impact: Double-disconnect, leaked tasks, inconsistent state machine. Fix: Use an asyncio.Lock or atomic state transitions.

docs.title: [Severity: P2] CI-V event queue overflow silently drops events
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py:1044-1051 Problem: Events silently dropped when queue full — no logging, no counter, no backpressure signal. Impact: Event consumers (web server, poller) miss critical state changes without notification. Fix: Add a dropped counter and log periodically.

docs.title: [Severity: P3] _civ_rx.py hard-codes IC-7610-specific routing tables
docs.text: File: src/icom_lan/_civ_rx.py:49-90 Problem: _CMD14_RECEIVER_LEVEL_FIELDS, _CMD16_RECEIVER_BOOL_FIELDS etc. are hard-coded dictionaries assuming IC-7610 sub-command layout rather than being data-driven from rig profiles. Impact: Adding support for a new Icom radio requires editing _civ_rx.py instead of just adding a TOML profile. Fix: Move routing tables into RadioProfile and look them up at runtime.

docs.title: Coverage Gaps (Critical)
docs.text:
1. 7 source modules have ZERO test files:
  2. radio_protocol.py (876 lines) — defines the core protocol interface
  3. backends/_icom_serial_base.py (590 lines) — serial backend base
  4. capabilities.py (195 lines) — capability registry
  5. meter_cal.py (117 lines) — meter calibration
  6. audio/lan_stream.py (703 lines) — LAN audio streaming
  7. backends/yaesu_cat/ (all modules) — entire Yaesu backend

  8. command_map.py (48 lines) — command mapping

  9. Transport failure scenarios untested — no tests for socket errors, handshake timeouts, invalid radio responses.

  10. Protocol edge cases missing — BCD boundary conditions (max digits, overflow), packet type identification for all 8+ types, sequence number wraparound at 0xFFFF.

  11. Pre-existing failing tests noted in MEMORY.md: TestBackpressure::test_scope_*, TestControlChannel::test_hello_capabilities_list, TestControlChannel::test_state_snapshot_fields.

docs.title: Flaky Patterns
docs.text:
1. Sleep-based timing in reconnect tests — test_reconnect.py uses asyncio.sleep(0.6) to wait for watchdog timeouts. Breaks under system load.

  2. Audio stress test uses real blocking sleep — test_audio_stress.py:109 calls time.sleep() inside callback, runs for 3+ real seconds.

  3. Thread contention tests not deterministic — test_audio_buffer_pool.py:125 uses time.sleep(0.001) hoping for race conditions.

docs.title: Anti-patterns
docs.text:
1. Mock-over-behavior — test_audio_recovery.py verifies mock call arguments but not that audio actually flows end-to-end.

  2. conftest.py monkey-patches _handle_raw_civ_packet — masks missing implementation with a no-op lambda.

  3. Integration tests require real hardware — 20 integration test files skip silently without physical radio. No mock equivalents for CI.

  4. MockTransport doesn't validate protocol — accepts any bytes, doesn't parse CI-V frames or simulate radio behavior.

docs.title: Test Quality Observations
docs.text:
• Protocol tests (test_protocol.py, test_commands.py) are excellent — testing real byte sequences against known values.
  • Transport tests properly mock UDP and verify handshake sequences.
  • Radio tests tend to mock many internal attributes, making them brittle to refactoring.
  • Performance regression tests have no stored baselines — assertions are loose (e.g., ops_per_sec > 50_000).

docs.title: Score Table
docs.text: Category Score Notes Module decomposition 5/10 commands.py (6.8K), radio.py (4.6K), handlers.py (2.4K), radio_poller._execute() (920 lines) are god modules. Good runtime decomposition pattern. API design 7/10 Capability protocol hierarchy is well-designed. But __init__.py exports 150+ symbols including internals. Concurrency safety 5/10 Fire-and-forget task in audio_bus, TOCTOU port race, unprotected state transitions, packet queue race, broad exception swallowing in RX pump. Locks used correctly where present. Error handling 5/10 12+ broad except Exception with DEBUG logging in critical CI-V path. 61 bare asserts. Good retry/backoff in reconnect. Silent event queue drops. Type safety 5/10 Good Protocol types. Weakened by Any in runtime protocols (10 fields), 32 dict[str, Any] in handlers, getattr defensiveness (110 occurrences). Test quality 5/10 Excellent protocol/command tests. 7 modules with zero coverage. Flaky sleep-based timing. Mock-over-behavior anti-pattern. No CI integration test equivalents. Overall 5.5/10 needs_attention — solid architectural vision with clean protocol hierarchy, but implementation has accumulated significant technical debt in module size, error handling, type safety, and test coverage.

docs.title: Immediate (next sprint)
docs.text:
1. Fix fire-and-forget task in AudioSubscription.stop() (P1, 5 min fix)
  2. Replace 61 assert resp is not None with proper CommandError raises (P1, mechanical)
  3. Promote CI-V exception logging from DEBUG to WARNING (P2, 30 min)
  4. Add dropped-event counter to _publish_civ_event (P2, 5 min)

docs.title: Short-term (next quarter)
docs.text:
1. Split commands.py into commands/ package by domain (P1, ~2 days)
  2. Extract facade classes from radio.py — start with scope, then audio (P1, ~1 week)
  3. Fix TOCTOU port allocation — pass bound sockets to transport (P1, ~2 hours)
  4. Reduce __init__.py exports to true public API (P1, ~1 day)
  5. Create test files for 7 untested modules (P0 test gap, ~3 days)
  6. Replace sleep-based test timing with deterministic clock injection (P0 flaky, ~2 days)

docs.title: Medium-term (next half)
docs.text:
1. Type _runtime_protocols.py fields properly, eliminate Any (P2)
  2. Create TypedDict for all WebSocket message schemas (P2)
  3. Add lock to connection state transitions (P2)
  4. Extract radio_poller._execute() into command dispatch table (P1)
  5. Create mock-integration equivalents for all hardware tests (P1)
  6. Make CI-V routing tables data-driven from rig profiles (P3)

docs.title: Session Report: DX Cluster + Power Control (2026-03-04)
Summary
docs.text:
Implemented DX cluster spot overlay on waterfall (issue #108) and power on/off button. Full Superpowers workflow test: brainstorming → planning → parallel agents → integration → live test.

Duration: ~2.5 hours (23:00 – 01:56 EST) Commits: 9 on feature/dx-cluster Lines: +1213 across 10 files Tests: 44 new, 0 failures

docs.title: DX Cluster (#108)
docs.text:
Backend (src/icom_lan/web/dx_cluster.py, 211 lines): - DXSpot — frozen dataclass (spotter, freq Hz, call, comment, time_utc, monotonic timestamp) - parse_spot(line) — regex parser for standard DX cluster format (DX de CALL: FREQ CALL comment time) - DXClusterClient — asyncio telnet client, sends callsign login, auto-reconnect with exponential backoff - SpotBuffer — deque ring buffer (max 200), band filtering (13 bands 160m–70cm), age expiry (default 30min), JSON serialization

Frontend (src/icom_lan/web/static/index.html, +271 lines): - Triangle markers on spectrum canvas with vertical dashed lines - Color aging: cyan (fresh) → gray (>15min) → removed (>30min) - DX toggle button in toolbar - Modal HTML badge on click: callsign (18px bold cyan), frequency, comment, spotter - Click badge → tune radio to spot frequency - Modal overlay prevents accidental waterfall clicks while badge is open

Integration: - WebSocket broadcast: dx_spot (single) and dx_spots (batch on connect) - REST endpoint: GET /api/v1/dx/spots - CLI flags: --dx-cluster HOST:PORT --callsign CALL (opt-in, disabled by default) - Server lifecycle: DX client starts/stops with WebServer

docs.title: Power On/Off
docs.text:
• set_powerstat(on: bool) added to Radio protocol (not just IcomRadio)
  • set_powerstat command in ControlHandler
  • ⏻ PWR button: red (OFF) / green (ON), browser confirm() before action
  • CI-V chain: button → WS → handler → radio.set_powerstat → CI-V 0x18 0x01/0x00 → IC-7610

docs.title: DX cluster in web/ subpackage, not core library
docs.text: Why: DX cluster ≠ radio control. Core library (icom_lan/) stays clean for radio protocol only. DX is a web UI feature. Tree-of-Thought analysis confirmed this.

docs.title: Backend required (no browser-only solution)
docs.text: Why: Browsers cannot open raw TCP sockets. Telnet to DX cluster server requires asyncio TCP client on backend.

docs.title: Hybrid parse-backend / filter-render-frontend
docs.text: Why: Backend parses raw telnet into structured DXSpot objects. Frontend filters by visible scope range and renders. Clean separation.

docs.title: Spots accepted even when DX toggle is OFF
docs.text: Why: Initial implementation only processed spots when DX was enabled → toggling ON showed nothing (spots arrived at WS connect when DX was off). Fix: always accumulate, display when enabled.

docs.title: Modal badge instead of canvas text
docs.text: Why: Canvas text at 8px was unreadable. HTML div overlay with proper CSS = readable at any zoom. Modal overlay prevents waterfall click-through.

docs.title: set_powerstat in Radio protocol
docs.text: Why: Power on/off is a fundamental radio function, not Icom-specific. Any backend (IC-7300 serial, Yaesu CAT) will need it. Must go through abstraction, not direct IcomRadio call.

docs.title: 1. DX spots ignored on WS connect
docs.text: Symptom: Toggle DX ON → nothing visible. Root cause: if (state.dxEnabled && msg.spots) — spots arrived before user enabled DX. Fix: Accept spots always; fetch via REST on toggle.

docs.title: 2. Wrong canvas element ID
docs.text: Symptom: Click on triangle → nothing happens. Root cause: Code referenced spectrumCanvas, actual ID is specCanvas. Fix: Correct element ID.

docs.title: 3. Pre-existing flaky test (TestAudioHandlerCodecDetection)
docs.text: Symptom: 4 tests fail in DX branch, pass in main. Root cause: MagicMock() fails isinstance(radio, AudioCapable) check in AudioBroadcaster._start_relay(). Tests in main passed due to execution order side-effect. Fix: MagicMock(spec=AudioCapable).

docs.title: 4. Zombie icom-lan processes
docs.text: Symptom: 5 processes from 9 PM fighting for radio → reconnect loop. Root cause: No stale PID check on startup. PID file written but never read. Fix: Manual kill. Issue needed for proper fix (flock or stale PID check).

docs.title: 5. sendCommand format in DX click-to-tune
docs.text: Symptom: Click-to-tune silently fails. Root cause: Used {cmd: 'set_freq', args: [freq]} instead of sendCommand('set_freq', {freq: freq}). Fix: Correct format.

docs.title: Workflow: Superpowers (parallel tmux agents)
docs.text:
First full test of the Superpowers skill (brainstorming → planning → parallel execution).

What worked: - Tree-of-Thought for architecture (backend vs frontend, where to place code) - 7-task plan committed as docs/plans/2026-03-04-dx-cluster.md - 2 parallel Claude Code agents in tmux (backend Tasks 1-3, frontend Task 6) - Backend: 17 min, 211 code + 375 tests, 3 TDD commits - Frontend: 4 min, 131 lines, 1 commit - Integration agent: 11 min, Tasks 4-5-7

What didn't work: - tmux send-keys with Enter unreliable for Claude Code TUI — text typed but not submitted - Fix: separate send-keys for text and Enter with sleep 1 between, or double Enter - Long prompts via file (/tmp/task.md), not inline send-keys - Backend agent tests hung (asyncio mock without timeout, 5GB RAM) — had to kill and let agent retry

Lesson: tmux agents are powerful but need babysitting. Enter key issue must be solved properly.

docs.text:

  • #114 — Enforce Radio protocol abstraction in web/ (ruff import ban + mypy)

docs.title: Before Epic #169:
docs.text:
• 5 critical bugs
  • No WS error handling
  • No reconnection UX
  • Visual flicker on freq tuning
  • Hardcoded IC-7610 assumptions
  • PTT could stick on WS disconnect
  • Missing UI panels
  • RAF loops ran when tab hidden

docs.title: After Epic #169:
docs.text:
• ✅ All P0/P1 bugs fixed
  • ✅ WS error responses logged
  • ✅ Reconnecting indicator + exponential backoff
  • ✅ Stale state warning (5s threshold)
  • ✅ PTT safety timeout (30s + auto-release)
  • ✅ Smooth freq tuning (1 Hz precision)
  • ✅ Capabilities-driven UI (multi-radio ready)
  • ✅ HTTP keepalive enabled
  • ✅ Settings/Antenna/Tuner/Split/AGC panels wired
  • ✅ Audio/Control WS coordination
  • ✅ RAF paused when tab hidden
  • ✅ App reload limited to 5 retries

docs.title: Overall Performance:
docs.text:
Total agents launched: 11 Successful: 8 (73%) Failed/Interrupted: 3 (27%)

Success by sprint: - Sprint 1: 4/6 (67%) - Sprint 2: 1/1 (100%) - Sprint 3: 3/4 (75%)

Completion times: - Fastest: 40s (raf-pause) - Slowest successful: 4m 30s (wire-panels) - Average: 2m 10s

Failed reasons: - freq-snap: Hung in "Determining" (5+ min) → manual fix - listener-leak: Correctly identified "already fixed" - reduce-ws: Too complex (16+ min, test suite timeout)

docs.title: ✅ What Worked
docs.text:
Agent orchestration: - Parallel agents = 6x speedup - tmux visibility = easy monitoring - Task files = reliable prompts - --dangerously-skip-permissions = no interruptions

Pre-verification: - Checking "already implemented" saved time - 6 issues were already fixed (#231, #232, #235, #236, #244, #245)

Focused prompts: - Short, specific tasks (1-2h max) worked best - Clear deliverables + commit message template

docs.title: ⚠️ What Didn't Work
docs.text:
Complex tasks: - Backend + frontend changes = too slow - Full test suite runs = timeouts - Multi-file refactors = agent confusion

Prompt delivery: - Long prompts via send-keys = unreliable - Need extra Enter key for Claude TUI - File-based prompts = more reliable

Timeouts: - No automatic timeout wrapper - Had to manually interrupt hung agents

docs.title: For Future Sprints:
docs.text:
1. Task sizing: - Keep agent tasks < 30 min expected - Split complex tasks into smaller pieces - Prefer frontend-only or backend-only (not both)

2. Agent monitoring: - Add 10-minute auto-interrupt for stuck agents - Use simpler progress detection (not just "Cooked") - Log agent output to files for post-mortem

3. Pre-flight checks: - Audit all issues before launch - Close "already implemented" first - Group similar tasks for same agent

4. Complexity filters: - Backend protocol changes → manual - Full test suite requirements → manual - Cross-cutting concerns (WS reduction) → manual

docs.title: #237 — Reduce WebSocket connections
docs.text: Effort: 4-6 hours (backend + frontend) Priority: Low (iOS 6-conn limit not hit yet) Approach: Merge meters into control WS

docs.title: #247 — LAN authentication
docs.text: Effort: 2 hours Priority: Low (LAN-only deployment) Approach: Optional token-based auth

docs.title: #248 — State delta / ETag 304
docs.text: Effort: 2 hours Priority: Low (state JSON only 2KB) Approach: ETag header + 304 responses

docs.title: v2 UI Manual Test Plan
docs.text: URL: http://192.168.55.152:8080?ui=v2 Prerequisites: IC-7610 connected, backend running Duration: 30-60 minutes

docs.title: Pre-Test Checklist
docs.text:
1. ✅ Backend running: ps aux | grep "icom-lan.*web"
  2. ✅ Radio connected: curl http://192.168.55.152:8080/api/v1/state | jq .main.freqHz
  3. 🌐 Open browser: http://192.168.55.152:8080?ui=v2
  4. 🔍 Open DevTools: F12 → Console tab (watch for errors)
  5. 🌐 Open Network tab: Monitor WebSocket connection + API calls

docs.title: 1. Initial Load & Layout ✅
docs.text:
Check: - [ ] Page loads without errors - [ ] Console shows no red errors - [ ] WebSocket connected: WS /api/v1/ws [101 Switching Protocols] - [ ] Three-column layout visible: Left sidebar, Spectrum, Right sidebar - [ ] Top VFO header visible with MAIN VFO - [ ] Bottom dock visible with meters

Expected: - Dark theme (bg #0B0E12) - Cyan accents (#00D4FF) - No white/blank screen - No "Failed to fetch" errors

If fails: Screenshot + console errors → create bug issue

docs.title: MAIN VFO Panel
docs.text:
Test: 1. [ ] Frequency display shows current freq (e.g., "7.175") 2. [ ] Mode shows (e.g., "USB") 3. [ ] Filter shows (e.g., "FIL1") 4. [ ] S-meter bar animates with signal 5. [ ] Badges visible (NB, NR, etc. if active)

Interact: - Click frequency → Does anything happen? (Pencil mockup shows edit modal) - Click mode → Mode selector? (Or nothing expected?)

Check Console: Any errors when clicking?

docs.title: SUB VFO Panel (if dual_rx)
docs.text: Test: - [ ] SUB VFO visible next to MAIN (IC-7610 has dual RX) - [ ] SUB freq/mode/filter different from MAIN - [ ] SUB S-meter works independently

docs.title: VFO Operations
docs.text:
Test buttons (between VFOs): 1. [ ] A↔B (Swap) → Click → Freqs swap? Console errors? 2. [ ] A→B (Copy) → Click → SUB copies MAIN? Errors? 3. [ ] SPLIT toggle → Click → Activates? Badge appears? 4. [ ] TX VFO selector → Switch between MAIN/SUB

Expected: Commands sent via WebSocket, optimistic UI update

Check Network tab: See {"type":"command","name":"vfo_swap"} etc.

docs.title: RF Front End
docs.text:
Test sliders: 1. [ ] RF Gain (0-255) → Drag → Value changes? Command sent? 2. [ ] ATT buttons (0/6/12/18 dB) → Click → Switches? Sends set_attenuator? 3. [ ] Preamp buttons (OFF/1/2) → Click → Works?

Console check: No errors? WebSocket commands visible?

docs.title: Filter Panel ⚠️ CRITICAL TEST
docs.text:
Test sliders: 1. [ ] Width (50-3600 Hz) → Drag → EXPECT ERROR (#304 bug!) - Console should show: ValueError: unhandled command: 'set_filter_width' - Document exact error message 2. [ ] IF Shift (-1200 to +1200 Hz) → Drag → Works? (Maps to PBT) 3. [ ] PBT Inner/Outer (if visible) → Drag → Works?

Expected: Width slider FAILS, others work.

docs.title: AGC Panel
docs.text: Test: 1. [ ] AGC Mode buttons (FAST/MID/SLOW) → Click → Switches? 2. [ ] AGC Gain slider → Drag → Value changes?

docs.title: RIT/XIT Panel
docs.text: Test: 1. [ ] RIT toggle → Click → Activates? Badge in VFO? 2. [ ] XIT toggle → Click → Activates? 3. [ ] Offset slider → Drag → RIT freq changes? 4. [ ] Clear button → Click → Resets to 0?

docs.title: Band Selector
docs.text:
Test: 1. [ ] Band buttons visible (160m, 80m, 40m, 30m, 20m, etc.) 2. [ ] Click "40m" → VFO jumps to 7 MHz? 3. [ ] Click "20m" → VFO jumps to 14 MHz?

Check: Frequency updates immediately (optimistic) then confirms

docs.title: RX Audio Panel
docs.text:
Test: 1. [ ] Monitor mode selector (local/live/mute) - Switch to "live" → Browser audio starts? - Switch to "mute" → Audio stops? 2. [ ] AF Level slider → Drag → Volume changes?

Expected: Live audio requires WebCodecs support (check browser support)

docs.title: DSP Panel
docs.text: Test: 1. [ ] NR toggle → Click → Badge appears in VFO? 2. [ ] NR Level slider → Drag → Works? 3. [ ] NB toggle → Click → Badge appears? 4. [ ] NB Level slider → Drag → Works? 5. [ ] Notch mode (off/auto/manual) → Click → Switches? 6. [ ] CW Pitch slider → Drag → Value changes?

docs.title: TX Panel
docs.text:
Test: 1. [ ] Mic Gain slider → Drag → Value updates? 2. [ ] ATU toggle → Click → Activates? (Careful: tunes antenna!) 3. [ ] ATU Tune button → Click → Starts tuning? (Don't press if on air!) 4. [ ] VOX toggle → Click → Activates? 5. [ ] COMP toggle → Click → Activates? 6. [ ] COMP Level slider → Drag → Works? 7. [ ] MON toggle → Click → Activates? 8. [ ] MON Level slider → Drag → Works?

WARNING: ATU Tune will actually tune antenna! Only test if safe.

docs.title: Receiver Summary Cards
docs.text: Test: 1. [ ] RX 1 (MAIN) card shows: - Current freq - Mode / Filter - S-meter bar (animates?) 2. [ ] RX 2 (SUB) card shows (if dual_rx): - SUB freq - SUB mode - SUB S-meter

docs.title: Meter Panel
docs.text:
Test: 1. [ ] Needle gauge visible (Power/SWR) 2. [ ] Bar gauges visible (Po/SWR/ALC) 3. [ ] Meter source selector → Switch S/SWR/POWER → Changes?

During TX (if safe): - [ ] Press PTT → Meters switch to TX mode (Power/SWR/ALC animate)

docs.title: 6. Spectrum & Waterfall 📡
docs.text:
Test: 1. [ ] Spectrum displays (top of center area) 2. [ ] Waterfall scrolls below spectrum 3. [ ] Click on waterfall → VFO tunes to clicked freq? 4. [ ] Passband overlay visible?

Expected: Existing v1 SpectrumPanel reused, should work

docs.title: 7. State Updates & Optimistic UI ⚡
docs.text:
Test live state sync:

  1. Change freq via v1 UI (open in another tab):
  2. Does v2 UI update automatically?
  3. Frequency display changes?

  4. Spectrum re-centers?

  5. Change mode via physical radio knob (if possible):

  6. Does v2 UI update?

  7. Mode label changes?

  8. Move RF Gain slider in v2:

  9. Does UI respond immediately (optimistic)?
  10. Or waits for backend confirmation?

Expected: Optimistic updates = instant UI response, backend confirms later

docs.title: 8. Capability Gating (IC-7300 simulation)
docs.text:
Cannot test directly without IC-7300, but check logic:

In browser console, run: 

// Check dual_rx capability
console.log(window.__RADIO_CAPS__?.capabilities?.includes('dual_rx'));

Expected: true for IC-7610, false for IC-7300

If false: SUB VFO should be hidden

docs.title: 9. Responsive Layout 📱
docs.text:
Test different window sizes:

  1. [ ] Desktop (1920×1080) → Three columns visible
  2. [ ] Tablet (1024×768) → Layout adapts?
  3. [ ] Mobile (375×667) → Mobile nav? (Or breaks?)

Known issue: #290-294 → VFO S-meter too small on 4K

Test on 4K (if available): - [ ] Open on 4K display - [ ] Is S-meter readable? Or too small? - [ ] Does layout look balanced?

docs.title: 10. Keyboard Shortcuts ⌨️
docs.text:
Test (if implemented): - [ ] Space → PTT (press & hold) - [ ] A → Switch to VFO A - [ ] B → Switch to VFO B - [ ] R → RIT toggle - [ ] T → Split toggle

Expected: May not be implemented yet (Phase 4 polish)

**URL:** http://192.168.55.152:8080?ui=v2
**Panel:** [e.g., FilterPanel]

**Expected:** [What should happen]
**Actual:** [What actually happened]

docs.title: Success Criteria
docs.text:
v2 UI is "working" if: - ✅ No console errors (except known #304) - ✅ All sliders send commands - ✅ State updates propagate to UI - ✅ Optimistic updates feel instant - ✅ VFO freq/mode/meter display correctly - ⚠️ Known issue: Filter width slider fails (#304) - ⚠️ Known issue: VFO S-meter small on 4K (#290-294)

v2 UI needs work if: - ❌ Multiple controls throw errors - ❌ State updates don't propagate - ❌ UI feels laggy/broken - ❌ Layout breaks on mobile

docs.title: After Testing
docs.text:
Create issues for: 1. Each unique console error (not #304) 2. Controls that don't send commands 3. Visual glitches (layout, colors, sizing) 4. State sync problems

Update audit report: - docs/reviews/2026-03-18-v2-ui-audit.md - Add "Runtime Testing Results" section - List confirmed bugs vs false positives

Comment on Epic #289: - Manual test complete ✅ - X bugs found - Y bugs are blockers - Z bugs are polish

# 3. Open browser

# 4. Open DevTools

# 5. Start testing!
